title: Scroll Panel
description: Scroll Panel provides a configurable scroll region with orientation, scrollbar visibility behavior, scroll events, and imperative scrolling methods for custom panel shells.
intro: Use `ui-scroll-panel` when you need a reusable scroll shell for custom layouts, panels, drawers, or card regions and do not want the higher-level data-loading behavior of `ui-scroll-container`.

featureSections.title: Basic scroll region
featureSections.description: The most common use case is a bounded vertical region inside a panel, drawer, or side layout.

featureSections.title: Horizontal and bidirectional layouts
featureSections.description: Different orientations matter when the content is a strip, grid, or canvas rather than a simple vertical list.

featureSections.title: Scrollbar visibility behavior
featureSections.description: Scrollbar behavior affects how heavy or minimal the shell feels. It should be chosen based on density and discoverability, not personal taste alone.

featureSections.title: Programmatic scrolling
featureSections.description: Imperative scrolling is useful when another shell action needs to move the user to the top, bottom, or far edge of a custom surface.

featureSections.title: Scroll and scroll-end events
featureSections.description: Scroll events are useful for analytics, shell reactions, and sticky behavior, especially when the panel is part of a more complex workspace.

featureSections.title: Inbox or side-panel composition
featureSections.description: A realistic scroll panel usually lives inside a denser shell with status, filters, or search rather than as an isolated box.

accessibility.title: Scrollable region semantics
accessibility.body: `ui-scroll-panel` renders a region with an accessible label. Supply a more specific `ariaLabel` when the generic scrollable-content wording is not enough for the surrounding UI.

accessibility.title: Keyboard behavior
accessibility.body:
Keyboard behavior mainly follows native scroll container expectations.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Tab / Shift+Tab | Moves focus into or past interactive content inside the panel. |
| Arrow keys / Page keys | Follow browser scrolling behavior when the region or an inner control has focus. |
| Home / End | Can move to the start or end of scrollable content depending on browser and focus context. |

accessibility.title: Bounded scroll areas
accessibility.body: Keep only the intended region scrollable. Nested scroll areas become hard to operate if the panel is not clearly bounded or if its label does not explain what the user is scrolling.

apiSections.title: ScrollPanelComponent - ui-scroll-panel
apiSections.lead: Standalone scroll shell component for custom layouts and bounded content regions. It supports multiple scroll orientations, scrollbar visibility modes, scroll events, and imperative scrolling methods.

blocks.title: Key inputs
rows: Maximum height of the scroll region.
Maximum width of the scroll region.
Accessible label for the scroll region.

blocks.title: Outputs and methods
rows: Emits while the user scrolls the panel.
Emits after scrolling settles, using a small debounce.
Imperatively scrolls to a given position.
Scrolls to the top of the panel.
Scrolls to the bottom of the panel.
Scrolls to the left edge of the panel.
Scrolls to the right edge of the panel.

paragraphs: Use `ui-scroll-panel` when you already control the content and only need the scroll shell. If you also need paged loading and row rendering, `ui-scroll-container` is the better fit.
Bidirectional scrolling should stay a deliberate choice. If users only need one axis, keep the other locked to reduce visual noise and interaction ambiguity.
